stage.subtitle_ar: هنا تتقن لغة التعامل مع المورّدين: تطلب القطعة الصحيحة، وتدير مخزونك بذكاء، وتفاوض على أفضل صفقة.

content.intro_ar: طلب قطع الغيار جزء يومي من عملك: قطعة ناقصة تعني سيارة واقفة وعميلًا ينتظر. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم الكلمات والجمل التي تحتاجها لتطلب القطع من المورّد بالإنجليزية بوضوح وثقة. تخيّل نفسك تتصل بالمورّد وتسأل عن التوفّر والسعر والتوصيل — هذا بالضبط ما ستتدرّب عليه.

vocab.meaning_en: one piece of a car or machine
vocab.example: I need a new part for this car.

vocab.meaning_en: to ask a company to send you something
vocab.example: I order parts from Riyadh every week.

vocab.meaning_en: a company that sells you the things you need
vocab.example: My supplier has a big shop in the industrial area.

vocab.meaning_en: the parts that press on the wheels to stop the car
vocab.example: These brake pads are old and weak.

vocab.meaning_en: the part that cleans the engine oil
vocab.example: Change the oil filter with every service.

vocab.meaning_en: extra, kept to use later
vocab.example: Keep a spare battery in the workshop.

vocab.meaning_en: bringing goods to your place
vocab.example: Delivery from this shop takes two hours.

vocab.meaning_en: a paper or file that shows the prices of all items
vocab.example: Please send me your new price list.

vocab.meaning_en: available in the shop now
vocab.example: Is this part in stock today?

vocab.meaning_en: made by the car's own company, not a copy
vocab.example: The customer wants an original part only.

paragraphs: Sameer has a workshop in the industrial area. Every morning, he checks the cars in the workshop. Today, a white Camry needs new brake pads and an oil filter. Sameer does not have these parts, so he calls his supplier.

The supplier's name is Abu Khalid. His shop is big, and his delivery is fast. Sameer asks, 'Are the brake pads for a 2020 Camry in stock?' Abu Khalid says, 'Yes, we have original brake pads. The price is 260 riyals.' Sameer also orders an oil filter and a spare battery.

Abu Khalid sends the new price list to Sameer's phone. The delivery arrives after two hours. Sameer checks every part before he pays. The parts are original, and the price is correct. Now the Camry is ready for work, and the customer is happy.

key_phrases.ar: هل هذه القطعة متوفّرة في المخزون؟

key_phrases.en: I need brake pads for a 2020 Camry.
How much is the original one?
Please send me your price list.
key_phrases.ar: من فضلك أرسل لي قائمة الأسعار.

comprehension.question: What does the white Camry need?
options: Brake pads and an oil filter
comprehension.correct: Brake pads and an oil filter
comprehension.explanation_ar: يذكر النص أن الكامري تحتاج إلى فحمات فرامل جديدة وفلتر زيت.

comprehension.question: Who is Abu Khalid?
comprehension.explanation_ar: أبو خالد هو المورّد الذي يتصل به سمير لطلب القطع.

comprehension.question: How much are the brake pads?
comprehension.explanation_ar: قال أبو خالد إن سعر فحمات الفرامل الأصلية ٢٦٠ ريالًا.

comprehension.question: When does the delivery arrive?
comprehension.explanation_ar: وصلت الطلبية بعد ساعتين من الاتصال.

comprehension.question: What does Sameer do before he pays?
comprehension.explanation_ar: يتحقق سمير من كل قطعة قبل أن يدفع، ليتأكد أنها أصلية وسليمة.

task.prompt_en: Write 3–4 sentences about the parts you often order for your business. Who is your supplier, and how fast is the delivery?
task.prompt_ar: اكتب ٣–٤ جُمل عن القطع التي تطلبها كثيرًا لعملك. من هو مورّدك؟ وكم يستغرق التوصيل؟
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل: supplier، in stock، delivery.

content.intro_ar: المخزون هو رأس مالك النائم على الرفوف: إذا زاد عن حاجتك جمّد أموالك، وإذا نقص أوقف شغلك. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم كيف تتحدّث بالإنجليزية عن المخزون والتوفّر وإعادة الطلب، وهي لغة ستحتاجها مع المورّدين ومع فريقك في الورشة. حاول وأنت تقرأ أن تربط كل مصطلح بواقع مستودعك أنت.

vocab.meaning_en: all the goods a business keeps, or the list of them
vocab.example: We check our inventory at the end of every month.

vocab.meaning_en: the goods you have ready to sell or use
vocab.example: Our stock of oil filters is getting low.

vocab.meaning_en: to have no more of something
vocab.example: We have run out of brake pads again.

vocab.meaning_en: a flat board where you store items
vocab.example: Put the new filters on the top shelf.

vocab.meaning_en: lines on a product that a machine can read
vocab.example: Scan the barcode when a part leaves the store.

vocab.meaning_en: written information that you keep about something
vocab.example: Keep a record of every part you use.

vocab.meaning_en: how much customers want something
vocab.example: Demand for batteries is high in summer.

vocab.meaning_en: selling very slowly
vocab.example: These old lamps are slow-moving items.

vocab.meaning_en: the stock level at which you order more
vocab.example: The reorder point for oil filters is ten.

vocab.meaning_en: to check how many items you have
vocab.example: We count the stock every Thursday.

vocab.meaning_en: a room where you keep parts and goods
vocab.example: The storeroom must stay clean and organized.

reading.title_en: Know What Is on Your Shelves
paragraphs: Many workshop owners lose money without knowing it. A car arrives, the mechanic needs a filter, and suddenly everyone discovers that the storeroom is empty. The job stops, the customer waits, and the part is bought quickly from another shop at a higher price. Good inventory control solves this problem before it happens.

Start with a simple record of your stock. Give every part a clear place on a shelf, and count your items on the same day every week. Many businesses now scan a barcode when a part enters or leaves the storeroom, so the numbers are always up to date. When you know your real numbers, you stop guessing.

Next, set a reorder point for your fast-moving parts. For example, if you usually use ten oil filters a week, order more when only fifteen are left. This way, you never run out of the parts that customers need most. Demand also changes with the seasons — batteries die in the hot summer, so smart owners increase their stock in May.

Finally, watch your slow-moving items. Parts that sit on the shelf for a year are money sleeping in your storeroom. Sell them at a discount, or stop ordering them. Your goal is simple: the right part, in the right place, at the right time.

key_phrases.en: Let me check if we have it in stock.
key_phrases.ar: دعني أتحقق إن كانت متوفّرة في المخزون.

key_phrases.en: We are running low on oil filters.
key_phrases.ar: مخزوننا من فلاتر الزيت بدأ ينفد.

key_phrases.en: We need to reorder before the weekend.
key_phrases.ar: نحتاج إلى إعادة الطلب قبل نهاية الأسبوع.

key_phrases.en: This item has been on the shelf for months.
key_phrases.ar: هذا الصنف على الرف منذ شهور.

key_phrases.en: Demand goes up in the summer.

comprehension.question: What problem does good inventory control solve?
options: Running out of parts during a job
comprehension.correct: Running out of parts during a job
comprehension.explanation_ar: ضبط المخزون الجيد يمنع توقّف العمل بسبب نفاد القطع في منتصف الإصلاح.

comprehension.question: Why do many businesses scan a barcode?
options: To keep stock numbers up to date
To make the storeroom look modern
comprehension.correct: To keep stock numbers up to date
comprehension.explanation_ar: مسح الباركود عند دخول القطعة أو خروجها يجعل الأرقام محدّثة دائمًا.

comprehension.question: In the example, when should you order more oil filters?
options: At the end of the year
comprehension.explanation_ar: المثال في النص: إذا كنت تستهلك عشرة فلاتر أسبوعيًا فاطلب المزيد عندما يتبقى خمسة عشر فقط.

comprehension.question: What happens to the demand for batteries in summer?
comprehension.explanation_ar: يرتفع الطلب على البطاريات في الصيف الحار، لذلك يزيد أصحاب الورش مخزونهم قبله.

comprehension.question: What does the writer say about slow-moving items?
options: They are money sleeping in your storeroom
They are the best parts to buy more of
They should be given to customers for free
comprehension.correct: They are money sleeping in your storeroom
comprehension.explanation_ar: يصف النص القطع بطيئة الحركة بأنها أموال نائمة في المستودع.

task.prompt_en: Write 4–5 sentences about how you organize the parts in your business. Which parts run out fastest, and when do you reorder them?
task.prompt_ar: اكتب ٤–٥ جُمل عن طريقتك في تنظيم القطع في عملك. أي القطع ينفد مخزونها أسرع؟ ومتى تعيد طلبها؟
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، مثل: stock، reorder point، storeroom، demand.

content.intro_ar: أنت تفاوض في سوق السيارات منذ سنوات، وتعرف قواعد اللعبة بالعربية — الجديد هنا أن تديرها بالإنجليزية بنفس الثقة. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّم لغة التفاوض مع المورّدين: طلب الخصم، وشروط الدفع، والضمان، والوصول إلى صفقة تكسب فيها أنت والمورّد معًا.

vocab.meaning_en: to discuss until you reach an agreement
vocab.example: I always negotiate before I sign a deal.

vocab.meaning_en: a lower price than usual
vocab.example: He gave me a 10% discount on the full order.

vocab.meaning_en: a written offer that shows the prices
vocab.example: Please send me a quotation for these parts.

vocab.meaning_en: the conditions of a deal, like price and payment time
vocab.example: We agreed on the terms after a long call.

vocab.meaning_en: a large amount bought at one time
vocab.example: Buying in bulk is usually cheaper.

vocab.meaning_en: the last day or time to do something
vocab.example: The delivery deadline is Thursday morning.

vocab.meaning_en: an agreement where each side accepts a little less
vocab.example: A good compromise keeps both sides happy.

vocab.meaning_en: continuing for a long time
vocab.example: I want a long-term relationship with my supplier.

vocab.meaning_en: a promise to repair or replace a product if it fails
vocab.example: These parts come with a one-year warranty.

vocab.meaning_en: a business agreement
vocab.example: We closed the deal in one meeting.

vocab.meaning_en: good for both sides
vocab.example: A win-win deal makes both businesses stronger.

reading.title_en: The Art of a Good Deal
paragraphs: Negotiating with suppliers is not a war; it is a conversation between two businesses that need each other. You need good parts at a fair price, and the supplier needs a customer who orders regularly and pays on time. When both sides understand this, the deal becomes much easier.

Before you call, prepare your numbers. Ask two or three companies for a quotation, so you know the real market price. Decide how much you will order — suppliers give a better discount when you buy in bulk. If you have bought from the same company for years, remind them: a loyal, long-term customer deserves special terms.

During the negotiation, talk about more than the price. Ask about the warranty on the parts, the delivery deadline, and the payment terms. Sometimes a supplier cannot lower the price, but he can offer free delivery or thirty days to pay. That is a compromise that saves you real money.

Finally, remember that the best deal is a win-win. If you push the supplier too hard, he may agree today and disappear tomorrow, or send you weaker parts. Be firm about your needs, be respectful about his, and build a relationship that serves your business for years.

key_phrases.en: Can you give me a better price on a bulk order?
key_phrases.ar: هل يمكنك إعطائي سعرًا أفضل على طلب كمية كبيرة؟

key_phrases.en: Does this part come with a warranty?
key_phrases.ar: هل تأتي هذه القطعة مع ضمان؟

key_phrases.en: I have been a loyal customer for five years.
key_phrases.ar: أنا عميل وفيّ لكم منذ خمس سنوات.

key_phrases.en: I will confirm the order today if we agree on delivery.
key_phrases.ar: سأؤكد الطلب اليوم إذا اتفقنا على التوصيل.

comprehension.question: According to the text, what is negotiation?
options: A conversation between two businesses that need each other
A way to trick the supplier
A test of who talks louder
comprehension.correct: A conversation between two businesses that need each other
comprehension.explanation_ar: يقول النص إن التفاوض حوار بين طرفين يحتاج كلٌّ منهما الآخر، وليس حربًا.

comprehension.question: Why should you ask several companies for a quotation?
options: To know the real market price
Because it is required by law
comprehension.correct: To know the real market price
comprehension.explanation_ar: طلب عروض أسعار من أكثر من شركة يجعلك تعرف السعر الحقيقي في السوق.

comprehension.question: What can a supplier offer if he cannot lower the price?
options: Free delivery or thirty days to pay
comprehension.correct: Free delivery or thirty days to pay
comprehension.explanation_ar: قد يعرض المورّد توصيلًا مجانيًا أو مهلة دفع ثلاثين يومًا كحلّ وسط.

comprehension.question: When do suppliers usually give a better discount?
options: When you are a new customer
comprehension.explanation_ar: يمنح المورّدون خصمًا أفضل عادةً عند الشراء بكميات كبيرة.

comprehension.question: What may happen if you push the supplier too hard?
options: He will always lower the price
He may send you weaker parts
He will offer a longer warranty
comprehension.correct: He may send you weaker parts
comprehension.explanation_ar: الضغط الزائد على المورّد قد يدفعه لإرسال قطع أضعف أو التوقف عن التعامل معك.

comprehension.question: What kind of deal does the writer recommend?
options: A deal where only you win
A deal that is good for both sides
comprehension.correct: A deal that is good for both sides
comprehension.explanation_ar: أفضل صفقة هي التي يكسب فيها الطرفان، لأنها تبني علاقة طويلة الأمد.

task.prompt_en: Imagine you are calling your supplier to negotiate a bulk order of brake pads. Record yourself: ask for a discount, ask about the warranty, and agree on a delivery deadline.
task.prompt_ar: تخيّل أنك تتصل بمورّدك للتفاوض على طلب كمية كبيرة من فحمات الفرامل. سجّل صوتك: اطلب خصمًا، واسأل عن الضمان، واتفق على موعد نهائي للتوصيل.
task.guidance_ar: استخدم ٤ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس، وتحدّث بجمل كاملة كأنك في مكالمة حقيقية.
